daily_brief: The Sudan war marks its grim third anniversary today as a Berlin donor conference convenes amid criticism from Khartoum, which called the gathering 'unacceptable' interference [France 24, The Guardian, Globe and Mail]. The conflict has killed an estimated 150,000 people and displaced 14 million, with UN officials expressing frustration at the lack of political urgency to end hostilities. Meanwhile, the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues to dominate global concerns, with the FAO warning that delayed shipments of fertilizer and fuel could trigger a global food crisis [ABC Australia], while France and the UK announced they will host talks Friday on a multinational mission to restore freedom of navigation [RFI]. US Treasury Secretary Bessent defended the Iran war's economic costs to the BBC, arguing short-term pain is worthwhile for long-term security [BBC]. In a diplomatic breakthrough, Israel and Lebanon held their first direct talks since 1993, mediated by Secretary of State Rubio in Washington [BBC, France 24]. China's President Xi met with Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov in Beijing, calling relations between the two powers 'precious' amid international instability [Al Jazeera].

top_stories.headline: Sudan War Enters Fourth Year as Berlin Donor Conference Meets Amid Khartoum's Criticism
bullets: The war between Sudan's army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) began on April 15, 2023, and has killed an estimated 150,000 people while displacing 14 million
Germany hosted a donor conference in Berlin on Wednesday to mobilize humanitarian aid; only 16% of the UN's $2.8 billion appeal has been funded this year
Sudan's government condemned the conference as 'surprising and unacceptable' interference, organized without consulting Khartoum
UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper announced doubling of British aid to £15 million for Sudanese emergency responders

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Emphasized UN official's frustration and criticism of international response as 'bloody unacceptable'

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Led with Sudan government's rejection of the conference

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focused on aid shortfalls and relief workers' desperation for global attention

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Ethiopian interference allegations and broader regional dynamics

top_stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon Hold First Direct Talks Since 1993 Amid Ongoing Hezbollah Conflict
bullets: Lebanese and Israeli officials met in Washington DC on Tuesday, mediated by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio
Both sides agreed to launch direct negotiations at a future date; Israel demanded disarmament of Hezbollah while Lebanon called for a ceasefire
Over 2,000 people have been killed since Israeli military operations in Lebanon began on March 2, 2026
Hezbollah claimed at least 24 attacks on Israel during the day of talks

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Straightforward diplomatic reporting with casualty figures

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Featured expert analysis emphasizing Hezbollah as hybrid actor complicating conventional diplomacy

top_stories.headline: France and UK to Host 40-Nation Talks on Multinational Mission to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
bullets: French President Macron and British PM Starmer will co-chair a video conference Friday with over 40 countries
The proposed mission would be 'purely defensive' to restore freedom of navigation once conflict conditions allow
FAO warned that critical agricultural inputs remain blocked, with 20-45% of key agrifood exports relying on the strait
Iran has demanded $270 billion in compensation for war damages and proposed a Hormuz transit tax as part of negotiations

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Focused on diplomatic coordination and French foreign minister statements

sources.name: ABC Australia
sources.framing_note: Emphasized agricultural and food security implications of blockade

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Iran's compensation demands and negotiating position

top_stories.headline: US Treasury Secretary Defends Iran War Costs, Says 'Bit of Pain' Worth Long-Term Security
bullets: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told BBC that short-term economic damage is acceptable to eliminate Iranian nuclear threat
IMF has warned the US-Israel war with Iran could push the global economy into recession
Bessent cited Iran's missile strike on Diego Garcia as evidence of threat to Western capitals
UK government stated there is 'no assessment' that Iran is trying to target Europe with missiles

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced reporting that included UK government pushback on threat assessment claims

top_stories.headline: Xi Jinping Meets Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov, Calls Relations 'Precious' Amid Global Instability
bullets: Chinese President Xi met Lavrov in Beijing's Great Hall of the People on Wednesday
Xi urged 'closer and stronger strategic coordination' between China and Russia
Xi called for both nations to 'firmly defend their legitimate interests and safeguard the unity of Global South countries'
Meeting comes amid fears Strait of Hormuz shutdown could trigger global food crisis affecting fertilizer and fuel exports

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Connected meeting to broader Iran war context and Global South positioning

additional_stories.headline: UK shelves Chagos Islands sovereignty treaty after Trump administration withdraws support
Spain approves plan to grant legal status to approximately 500,000 undocumented migrants
Trump administration asks appeals court to vacate seditious conspiracy convictions of Oath Keepers and Proud Boys leaders
Super Typhoon Sinlaku batters Northern Mariana Islands with 240 km/h winds, strongest storm on Earth this year
Nigerian military airstrike on Jilli market kills as many as 200 people, including civilians, while targeting jihadist group
DRC peace talks open in Geneva as M23 fighting shifts to remote highland areas
About 250 Rohingya and Bangladeshi migrants missing after boat capsizes in Andaman Sea
JD Vance defends campaign visit supporting Orbán days before Hungarian PM's landslide election defeat
Australian PM Albanese visits Brunei seeking energy supply guarantees amid Iran war disruptions
Sixteen injured after former student opens fire at high school in southeastern Turkey before killing himself
